perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to others, using the same degree of care used to protect its own confidential or proprietary information of like importance, but in any case using no less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall endeavor to protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to others.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall protect" to "shall endeavor to protect", the obligation on the Recipient to protect the Confidential Information is significantly weakened. This creates an in-text contradiction with section 11 which states "The Parties acknowledge that Confidential Information is unique and valuable, and that disclosure in breach of this Agreement may result in irreparable injury to Owner for which monetary damages alone may not be an adequate remedy.". This contradiction creates uncertainty of enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge that Confidential Information is unique and valuable, and that disclosure in breach of this Agreement may result in irreparable injury to Owner for which monetary damages alone may not be an adequate remedy. Therefore, the Parties agree that in the event of a breach or threatened breach of confidentiality, the Owner shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach or anticipated breach without the necessity of posting a bond. Any such relief shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of monetary damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated "The Recipient shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to others", which is a clear obligation. The changed text modifies this to "The Recipient shall endeavor to protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to others", weakening the obligation. The contradicted text states "The Parties acknowledge that Confidential Information is unique and valuable, and that disclosure in breach of this Agreement may result in irreparable injury to Owner...", which emphasizes the importance and potential harm of a breach. Because the change weakens the protection obligation, it creates a contradiction with the contradicted text's assumption that confidential information will be strongly protected, thus creating uncertainty regarding enforcement and remedies.

perturbation.original_text: All such Confidential Information in tangible form shall be returned to the Owner promptly upon written request or the termination or expiration of this Agreement, and shall not thereafter be retained in any form by the Recipient, its Affiliates, or any employees or independent contractors of the Recipient or its Affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: All such Confidential Information in tangible form should be considered for return to the Owner upon written request or the termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall be returned" to "should be considered for return," the mandatory obligation to return tangible confidential information is removed. This creates an in-text contradiction with section 9 (c), which states "Each party agrees to protect Confidential Information received from the other by exercising, at a minimum, the same level of care it uses to protect its own confidential information and agrees that it will (c) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third person, except that Confidential Information may be disclosed to the demand of the Government.". If the tangible form of Confidential Information isn't returned, the confidential information may be disclosed to a third person.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees to protect Confidential Information received from the other by exercising, at a minimum, the same level of care it uses to protect its own confidential information and agrees that it will (a) use such Confidential Information only for the purposes of this Agreement; (b) only make such Confidential Information available to its employees having a “need to know”; (c) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third person, except that Confidential Information may be disclosed to the demand of the Government (d) not copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, except as necessary to carry out the purposes of this Agreement, pr ovided that, if they do copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, the reproduction shall carry a proprietary notice or legend similar to that which appears on the original; (e) not manufacture, sell, use, or have made devices embodying or utilizing Confidential In formation, except as otherwise agreed to by the Parties in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation to return confidential information from "shall be returned" to "should be considered for return." This directly contradicts section (c) of the contradicted text, which mandates protecting confidential information and not disclosing it to third parties. By removing the mandatory return, the risk of disclosure increases, creating a clear conflict between the altered obligation and the explicit requirement to prevent disclosure. Therefore, the change causes a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall also certify to the Owner the timely destruction of all copies of Confidential Information and all notes and memoranda, in whatever form maintained, incorporating any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may, at its discretion, certify to the Owner the destruction of copies of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall certify" to "may, at its discretion, certify", the requirement for the recipient to certify the destruction of confidential information becomes optional. This creates an in-text contradiction with section 6 (All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement (including without limitation, information in computer software or held in electronic storage media) shall be and remain the property of the Owner.), because the owner doesn't know if the confidential information, its remain his property or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement (including without limitation, information in computer software or held in electronic storage media) shall be and remain the property of the Owner. All such Confidential Information in tangible form shall be returned to the Owner promptly upon written request or the termination or expiration of this Agreement, and shall not thereafter be retained in any form by the Recipient, its Affiliates, or any employees or independent contractors of the Recipient or its Affiliates. The Recipient shall also certify to the Owner the timely destruction of all copies of Confidential Information and all notes and memoranda, in whatever form maintained, incorporating any Confidential Information. The Parties’ representatives primarily responsible for coordinating disclosure and receipt of Confidential Information and further responsible for destruction and/or return of Confidential Information, upon termination of this Agreement are: If to Amphenol: N a m e : T i t l e : A d d r e s s : Telephone: F a c s i m i l e : If to: N a m e : T i t l e : A d d r e s s : Telephone: F a c s i m i l e :
perturbation.justification: The original text stated "The Recipient shall also certify to the Owner the timely destruction of all copies of Confidential Information", which is a mandatory action. The changed text modifies this to "The Recipient may, at its discretion, certify to the Owner the destruction of copies of Confidential Information", making the certification optional. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which states "All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement (including without limitation, information in computer software or held in electronic storage media) shall be and remain the property of the Owner". The owner can't be sure that the confidential information is still its property because the certification is optional. The fact that the Recipient is no longer required to certify the destruction creates a strong contradiction with the section stating the confidential information remains the property of the owner, thus causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees to protect Confidential Information received from the other by exercising, at a minimum, the same level of care it uses to protect its own confidential information and agrees that it will (a) use such Confidential Information only for the purposes of this Agreement; (b) only make such Confidential Information available to its employees having a “need to know”; (c) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third person, except that Confidential Information may be disclosed to the demand of the Government (d) not copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, except as necessary to carry out the purposes of this Agreement, pr ovided that, if they do copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, the reproduction shall carry a proprietary notice or legend similar to that which appears on the original; (e) not manufacture, sell, use, or have made devices embodying or utilizing Confidential In formation, except as otherwise agreed to by the Parties in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees to protect Confidential Information received from the other and agrees that it will (a) use such Confidential Information only for the purposes of this Agreement; (b) only make such Confidential Information available to its employees having a “need to know”; (c) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third person, except that Confidential Information may be disclosed to the demand of the Government (d) not copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, except as necessary to carry out the purposes of this Agreement, pr ovided that, if they do copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, the reproduction shall carry a proprietary notice or legend similar to that which appears on the original; (e) not manufacture, sell, use, or have made devices embodying or utilizing Confidential In formation, except as otherwise agreed to by the Parties in writing.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "by exercising, at a minimum, the same level of care it uses to protect its own confidential information", it changes the level of protection afforded to the Confidential Information. This creates an in-text contradiction with section 11 which states "The Parties acknowledge that Confidential Information is unique and valuable, and that disclosure in breach of this Agreement may result in irreparable injury to Owner for which monetary damages alone may not be an adequate remedy.". This contradiction creates uncertainty of enforcement, since the level of care is no longer clear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge that Confidential Information is unique and valuable, and that disclosure in breach of this Agreement may result in irreparable injury to Owner for which monetary damages alone may not be an adequate remedy. Therefore, the Parties agree that in the event of a breach or threatened breach of confidentiality, the Owner shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach or anticipated breach without the necessity of posting a bond. Any such relief shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of monetary damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a minimum level of care for protecting confidential information, "at a minimum, the same level of care it uses to protect its own confidential information." The changed text removes this specific level of care. The contradicted text emphasizes the unique and valuable nature of confidential information, stating that a breach may cause irreparable injury. By removing the standard of care, the modified text introduces uncertainty regarding the protection of confidential information, which conflicts with the intention of the contradicted text to protect said information. This uncertainty weakens the enforceability of the confidentiality clause, as there is no longer a clear standard to measure a breach against. Therefore, a contradiction exists, since the level of enforcement becomes unclear as a result of the change.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties acknowledge that Confidential Information is unique and valuable, and that disclosure in breach of this Agreement may result in irreparable injury to Owner for which monetary damages alone may not be an adequate remedy. Therefore, the Parties agree that in the event of a breach or threatened breach of confidentiality, the Owner shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach or anticipated breach without the necessity of posting a bond.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties acknowledge that Confidential Information is valuable. Therefore, the Parties agree that in the event of a breach or threatened breach of confidentiality, the Owner shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach or anticipated breach without the necessity of posting a bond.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'unique and that disclosure in breach of this Agreement may result in irreparable injury to Owner for which monetary damages alone may not be an adequate remedy', the emphasis on the irreplaceable nature of the information is removed. This weakens the basis for seeking equitable relief and contradicts with section 9 (Each party agrees to protect Confidential Information received from the other by exercising, at a minimum, the same level of care it uses to protect its own confidential information).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees to protect Confidential Information received from the other by exercising, at a minimum, the same level of care it uses to protect its own confidential information and agrees that it will (a) use such Confidential Information only for the purposes of this Agreement; (b) only make such Confidential Information available to its employees having a “need to know”; (c) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third person, except that Confidential Information may be disclosed to the demand of the Government (d) not copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, except as necessary to carry out the purposes of this Agreement, pr ovided that, if they do copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, the reproduction shall carry a proprietary notice or legend similar to that which appears on the original; (e) not manufacture, sell, use, or have made devices embodying or utilizing Confidential In formation, except as otherwise agreed to by the Parties in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text highlights the 'unique and valuable' nature of confidential information and the potential for 'irreparable injury' if breached, justifying equitable relief. The changed text removes this emphasis, stating only that the information is 'valuable.' This weakening of the justification for equitable relief contradicts the contradicted text (Each party agrees to protect Confidential Information received from the other by exercising, at a minimum, the same level of care it uses to protect its own confidential information), because it undermines the obligation to protect the confidential information with a high level of care, as the severity of the repercussions of a breach are less emphasized.
